author: jsign
content: The `NewPayloadRequest` is something defined in consensus-specs such that the hash_tree_root matches what the CL expects, not strictly EngineAPI definitions

author: jsign
content: This was a recent fix here https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/pull/2761

author: jsign
content: lmk if htis makes sense to you

author: 0xrubo
content: to me, it would make more sense to keep zkevm input aligned with the engine api ssz format, so we use the same code

author: 0xrubo
content: so far, this is the only one diverging

author: 0xrubo
content: if engine api and zkevm data structures are gonna diverge more, that is one thing. if not, better to keep aligned.

author: jsign
content: Yeah they not being exactly the same is some tension that if you want to simplify the EL, you will ask the CL to have the other view for public input verification (since symmetrically they should know hte extra engine api format). so i think there might not be a free lunch solution

author: jsign
content: cc <@402080271563751435> if he might have an opinion from the CL side reg specs... mostly reg `NewPayloadRequest` might survive only for hte context of proving, so maybe there is some angle of potential justification on which side could be more flexible. although all this is might be quite subjective

author: jsign
content: i agree that the only field that has this divergence is the current one, maybe we can leave things as they are since is not a big difference for ELs to handle. but still agree worth raising this point to be aware; and maybe at some fork boundary the diff might be bigger and we can reevaluate. (thinking out loud here)

author: 0xrubo
content: <@774033563732541451> are there any test vectors for statelessinput for the latest changes?

author: jsign
content: Planning to do a new release soon on top of [bal@v7.0.0](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-spec-tests/releases/tag/bal%40v7.0.0)

author: jsign
content: <@912415191067676693> is releasing on top of bal@v7.0.0 sounds good to you? We're keeping the "policy" of targeting teh latest BAL release since prob most ELs are moving their main working branch there. (cc <@761154847432769577> <@510413979080720385>  <@281967554572976128> <@373533774752907275> )

author: taulepton_
content: Currently the stateless input uses the ssz version right? I think this is preferable. If I'm not mistaken the other representation is an artefact of the EL not having SSZ. Given that some EL's are now moving to support SSZ (I believe) we may consider changing the engineAPI to have a unified SSZ based standard.

author: jsign
content: I'm not sure the EngineAPI representation is related to SSZ not present in the EL, since the bytes are mainly a more neutral encoding of requests (~`TYPE_BYTE || concat(serialize(item) for item)`).   I suspect this was done since the thing the EL does in the EngineAPI is just sha256-it as is to put it as block header field for later verification.

author: jsign
content: but i agree we can wait a bit to see what happens with SSZ efforts in EngineAPI and see then if anything about this is changed

author: jsign
content: Thinking further: maybe is because if we add new types for requests, the EngineAPI remains quite stable since receives bytes and the only thing it does is sha256 so that's enough

author: 0xrubo
content:
> the only thing it does is sha256 so that's enough
right, that's why bytes are opaque to exec client. that is why i wanted to have them the same for guest input but maybe they will come in handy in the future. you may know more here.

author: 0xrubo
content: yes, it is. i assume you release them tomorrow for the breakout

author: jsign
content: I've to do some rebasings on some branchings and tests things a bit. But will be tomorrow (not sure exactly if before or after the breakout)

author: jsign
content: No extra insight from my side apart from just seeing it as a CL / EL  (verifier vs prover) preference (something out of ordinary is pushed to some side in either case).

author: 0xrubo
content: yeah, unification is quite welcome

author: taulepton_
content:
I think this EIP provides the motivation behind the encoding standard:
```
By having the bytes of request_data array from second byte on be opaque bytes, rather than an RLP (or other encoding) list, we can support different encoding formats for the request payload in the future such as SSZ, LEB128, or a fixed width format.
```

https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7685

author: 0xrubo
content: correct

author: taulepton_
content: Looks as if maybe there was already some expectation to move to SSZ in the future

author: jsign
content: Wise people 😄
